proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_enzalutamide.
Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_abiraterone.
Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_docetaxel.
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib.
Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_lu177_psma.
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 36.1% on enzalutamide vs 15.9% off (n=20076 vs 29924). RD=+0.202.
Response rate 23.8% on abiraterone vs 24.1% off. RD=-0.003 (n=14991 vs 35009).
Response rate 24.0% on docetaxel vs 24.0% off. RD=-0.0003.
Response rate 23.9% on olaparib vs 24.0% off. RD=-0.001.
Response rate 24.3% on lu177-PSMA vs 24.0% off. RD=+0.003.
Response rate 23.9% on pembrolizumab vs 24.0% off. RD=-0.002.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have a different rate of objective_response than patients with brca2_mutation=0.
Patients with ar_v7_positive=1 have a lower rate of objective_response than patients with ar_v7_positive=0.
Patients with msi_high=1 have a different rate of objective_response than patients with msi_high=0.
Patients with psma_high=1 have a different rate of objective_response than patients with psma_high=0.

analyses.result_summary: Response 15.0% in BRCA2+ vs 25.0% in BRCA2- (n=4996 vs 45004). RD=-0.101.
Response 17.6% in MSI-high vs 24.2% in MSI-stable (n=1528 vs 48472). RD=-0.066.
Response 23.9% in PSMA-high vs 24.3% in PSMA-low. RD=-0.004.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with mcrpc=1 (castration-resistant disease) have a lower rate of objective_response than patients with mcrpc=0.
Patients with visceral_mets=1 have a lower rate of objective_response than patients with visceral_mets=0.

analyses.result_summary: Response 15.4% in mCRPC vs 34.6% in non-mCRPC (n=27481 vs 22519). RD=-0.192.
Response 24.0% with visceral mets vs 24.0% without. No marginal effect (RD=-0.001).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response rate.
Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with lower objective_response rate.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response rate.
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response rate.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response rate.
Age (age_years) is associated with objective_response rate.
Higher gleason_score is associated with lower objective_response rate.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate logistic: coef=-0.285, OR=0.752 per unit ECOG.
Univariate logistic: coef=-0.0047 per ng/ml, OR=0.995. Higher PSA correlates with lower response.
Univariate logistic: coef=+0.096 per g/dL, OR=1.10.
Univariate logistic: coef=-0.032 per %, OR=0.969.
Univariate logistic: coef=-0.0069 per mg/L, OR=0.993.
Univariate logistic: coef=+0.0002 per year, essentially zero effect.
Univariate logistic: coef=+0.005 per Gleason point, no effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for prognostic features and other treatments, treatment_enzalutamide remains associated with higher objective_response.
After adjusting for prognostic features and other treatments, abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177_psma, and pembrolizumab show no main-effect benefit on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted enzalutamide OR=3.32 (coef=+1.20) controlling for other treatments and prognostic factors (age, ECOG, PSA, mCRPC, visceral_mets, albumin, CRP, weight loss, BRCA2, AR-V7, MSI, PSMA).
Adjusted ORs: abiraterone 0.97 (p=0.31), docetaxel 1.00 (p=0.94), olaparib 1.03 (p=0.51), lu177-PSMA 1.03 (p=0.36), pembrolizumab 1.01 (p=0.79). All null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within brca2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_olaparib produces a higher objective_response rate than no olaparib.
Within msi_high=1 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab produces a higher objective_response rate than no pembrolizumab.
Within psma_high=1 patients, treatment_lu177_psma produces a higher objective_response rate than no lu177-PSMA.

analyses.code: Logit interaction: y ~ olaparib + brca2 + olaparib:brca2; stratified RD in brca2=1.
analyses.result_summary: Within BRCA2+ (n=4996), olaparib response 12.1% vs 15.3% off; RD=-0.032 (p=0.06). Interaction coef=-0.289 (p=0.045). Direction is NEGATIVE, refuting the canonical PARP-inhibitor hypothesis in this dataset.

analyses.code: Stratified TE: chi2 on (pembro, response) within msi_high=1
analyses.result_summary: Within MSI-high (n=1528), pembro response 17.7% (n=79) vs 17.6% off (n=1449); RD=+0.001 (p=1.00). No effect of pembrolizumab even in MSI-high.

analyses.code: Stratified TE in psma_high=1; interaction model
analyses.result_summary: Within PSMA-high (n=29962), lu177 response 23.8% vs 23.9% off; RD=-0.001 (p=0.88). Interaction with psma_high p=0.36. No PSMA-defined targeted benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is attenuated (interaction) in patients with ar_v7_positive=1 compared to ar_v7_positive=0.
The effect of treatment_abiraterone on objective_response is attenuated in patients with ar_v7_positive=1 compared to ar_v7_positive=0.

analyses.result_summary: Strong negative interaction. Among AR-V7- (n=39962): enza 41.1% vs 16.0% off, RD=+0.251. Among AR-V7+ (n=10038): enza 16.5% vs 15.7% off, RD=+0.008. Interaction coef=-1.237.
Among AR-V7- (n=39962): abi 25.7% vs 26.2% off, RD=-0.005. Among AR-V7+ (n=10038): abi 16.3% vs 15.8% off, RD=+0.005. No interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is attenuated in patients with mcrpc=1 compared to mcrpc=0.
The effect of treatment_abiraterone on objective_response is attenuated in patients with mcrpc=1 compared to mcrpc=0.
The effect of treatment_docetaxel on objective_response is attenuated in patients with mcrpc=1 compared to mcrpc=0.

analyses.result_summary: Massive negative interaction. Among non-mCRPC (n=22519): enza 61.0% vs 16.9% off, RD=+0.440. Among mCRPC (n=27481): enza 15.8% vs 15.0% off, RD=+0.008. Interaction coef=-1.978.
Among non-mCRPC: abi 33.9% vs 34.9% off, RD=-0.010. Among mCRPC: abi 15.4% vs 15.3% off, RD=+0.001. No interaction.
Among non-mCRPC: doc 34.8% vs 34.5% off, RD=+0.003. Among mCRPC: doc 15.2% vs 15.4% off, RD=-0.001. No interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is concentrated in patients who are simultaneously mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0; in any other combination of these two factors the effect is null.

analyses.code: Stratified by 4 cells of mcrpc x ar_v7_positive

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is heterogeneous across binary biomarkers and clinical features; specifically brca2_mutation, msi_high, and psma_high may modify it.

analyses.code: interaction_test(df, 'treatment_enzalutamide', f) for each binary feature f
analyses.result_summary: Significant negative enzalutamide-by-feature interactions: mcrpc (coef=-1.98, p<1e-300), ar_v7_positive (coef=-1.24, p=1.4e-93), brca2_mutation (coef=-1.14, p=3.3e-42), msi_high (coef=-1.28, p=1.8e-19). visceral_mets and psma_high show no significant interaction (p=0.86 and 0.40). All four biomarker-driven negative modifiers blunt the enzalutamide effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is also modified by continuous prognostic features (PSA, ECOG, albumin, etc.) when split at median.

analyses.code: interaction_test(df, 'treatment_enzalutamide', f_above_median) for each continuous feature f
analyses.result_summary: Only psa_ng_ml above median (interaction coef=-0.91, p=2.3e-94) and ecog_ps above median (coef=+0.25, p=2.3e-4) show significant median-split interactions. PSA effect is largely confounded with mCRPC (PSA is much higher in mCRPC). Other 17 continuous variables show no interaction (all p>0.05).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the non-mCRPC AND non-AR-V7+ subgroup, treatment_enzalutamide retains a strong objective_response effect ONLY in patients with brca2_mutation=0; among brca2_mutation=1 patients in this subgroup the effect is null.

Within the non-mCRPC AND non-AR-V7+ subgroup, treatment_enzalutamide retains a strong objective_response effect ONLY in patients with msi_high=0; among msi_high=1 patients in this subgroup the effect is null.
Within the non-mCRPC AND non-AR-V7+ subgroup, the treatment_enzalutamide effect is similar in psma_high=1 and psma_high=0 patients.

analyses.code: interaction_test on subset where mcrpc=0 & arv7=0
analyses.result_summary: BRCA2+ within subgroup (n=1825): enza 15.3% vs 14.9%, RD=+0.004. BRCA2- within subgroup (n=16175): enza 78.0% vs 17.3%, RD=+0.607. Interaction coef=-2.80, p=6.6e-90. BRCA2 status fully ablates the enzalutamide effect even within the otherwise-responsive subgroup.

analyses.code: interaction_test on subset where mcrpc=0 & arv7=0
analyses.result_summary: MSI-high within subgroup (n=544): enza 18.5% vs 20.7%, RD=-0.022. MSI-stable within subgroup (n=17456): enza 73.3% vs 17.0%, RD=+0.563. Interaction coef=-2.74, p=9.6e-34.

analyses.code: interaction_test on subset where mcrpc=0 & arv7=0
analyses.result_summary: PSMA-high (n=10836): RD=+0.531. PSMA-low (n=7164): RD=+0.568. Interaction coef=-0.21, p=0.006 — direction is small and the effect is large in both strata, so PSMA does not gate the enza response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_enzalutamide produces a large positive objective_response benefit specifically in patients who are mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0; in patients who fail any one of these four predicates, the effect of enzalutamide is null.

analyses.code: Stratified TE in 4-criterion subgroup vs complement
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup (n=15681, 31% of cohort): enza 79.8% vs 17.2% off, RD=+0.626, p<1e-300. Complement (n=34319): enza 16.1% vs 15.3% off, RD=+0.008, p=0.06. Effect is essentially confined to this 4-criterion subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The enzalutamide benefit in the four-criterion subgroup remains large after adjusting for other prognostic features (age, PSA, ECOG, albumin, CRP, weight loss, visceral mets, PSMA, Gleason) and other concurrent treatments.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted enzalutamide coefficient in the four-criterion subgroup: +3.02, OR=20.5, p<1e-300. Effect size strengthens slightly after adjustment, confirming independence from other prognostic factors and concurrent therapies.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_abiraterone effect on objective_response is heterogeneous across patient features; in particular, abiraterone may have a positive effect in some subgroup not visible in the marginal analysis.

analyses.code: Full enumerated logistic interactions of abiraterone with each binary and median-split continuous feature.
analyses.result_summary: Of ~25 features tested, only abiraterone:brca2_mutation reached p<0.05 (coef=+0.19, p=0.035) — likely chance given multiple testing. Stratified TEs show no clinically meaningful subgroup: BRCA2+ stratum RD=+0.012 (n=4996, p=0.49). No subgroup with appreciable benefit identified.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_docetaxel effect on objective_response is heterogeneous across patient features and may be larger in specific subgroups.

analyses.result_summary: Of ~25 features tested, no binary modifier reached p<0.05; median-split alkaline_phosphatase (p=0.033) and alt (p=0.049) are nominally significant but isolated and small (coef magnitudes <0.1). Stratified TEs do not reveal any subgroup with appreciable docetaxel benefit. Docetaxel main effect remains null in every plausible subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_olaparib effect on objective_response is heterogeneous across patient features; in particular it may be different in BRCA2+ patients and may interact with renal/hepatic labs.

analyses.code: interaction_test(df, 'treatment_olaparib', f) and stratified TE within brca2=1, mcrpc subsets
analyses.result_summary: Olaparib:brca2 interaction p=0.045 with NEGATIVE coef (-0.29). Stratified: BRCA2+ olaparib RD=-0.032 (p=0.06); BRCA2+ AND mCRPC RD=-0.041 (p=0.07); BRCA2+ AND non-mCRPC RD=-0.021 (p=0.46). No subgroup shows benefit; the canonical PARP-inhibitor hypothesis is refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_lu177_psma effect on objective_response varies by visceral_mets; specifically it may be lower (or even harmful) in visceral_mets=1 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Lu177:visceral_mets interaction coef=-0.19, p=0.010. visceral_mets=0 stratum (n=40027): RD=+0.010 (p=0.10). visceral_mets=1 stratum (n=9973): RD=-0.024 (p=0.043). The 'benefit' of lu177 is at most a few percentage points in non-visceral disease, and a small detriment in visceral disease. No clinically meaningful positive subgroup identified.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_pembrolizumab effect on objective_response varies by patient features (CRP, albumin, alkaline phosphatase) and may show a positive subgroup.

analyses.result_summary: Marginally significant interactions: pembro:crp_hi coef=-0.27 (p=0.008), pembro:albumin_hi coef=+0.20 (p=0.041); not robust after multiple testing. Stratified TE in MSI-high subgroup (n=1528): RD=+0.001 (p=1.00). No subgroup with appreciable pembrolizumab benefit identified.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A logistic model that includes each treatment with main-effect terms and interaction terms with every prognostic feature reproduces the heterogeneity profile: enzalutamide has many strong feature interactions, while abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177-PSMA, and pembrolizumab have few or none.

analyses.code: For each treatment t: Logit(y, [t] + features + [t*f for f in features])
analyses.result_summary: Enzalutamide: 4 highly significant interactions (mcrpc p<1e-300, brca2 p=6e-67, arv7 p=3e-139, msi p=2e-27). Abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177-PSMA, pembrolizumab: at most 1 nominally significant interaction each, all with small coefficients (|coef|<=0.3). The signal of treatment-effect heterogeneity is overwhelmingly enzalutamide-specific.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the enzalutamide-responder subgroup (mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0), abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177-PSMA, and pembrolizumab provide no additional benefit beyond enzalutamide.

analyses.result_summary: On responder subgroup (n=15681): enza coef=+2.95 (p<1e-300, OR=19.2). Other treatments: abi -0.07 (p=0.11), doc +0.07 (p=0.12), olap -0.01 (p=0.93), lu177 -0.01 (p=0.93), pembro +0.03 (p=0.76). Only enzalutamide produces benefit in this subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Outside the enzalutamide-responder subgroup, none of the six treatments (enzalutamide, abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177-PSMA, pembrolizumab) provides a meaningful benefit.

analyses.result_summary: On non-responder subgroup (n=34319): all treatment coefficients are tiny: enza +0.06 (p=0.06), abi 0.00 (p=0.92), doc -0.02 (p=0.56), olap 0.00 (p=0.94), lu177 +0.05 (p=0.27), pembro +0.08 (p=0.25). No treatment produces appreciable benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A regression tree fit to the Tian-style transformed-outcome pseudo-response (response * (T - p_T)/(p_T*(1-p_T))) for treatment_enzalutamide partitions patients by mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, and brca2_mutation, with leaves having large positive enzalutamide effects (RD>+0.5) iff mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0.

analyses.code: DecisionTreeRegressor(max_depth=4, min_samples_leaf=1000) on Tian pseudo-outcome with 18 features
analyses.result_summary: Tree's first three splits are mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation. Leaves where all three are 0 have enzalutamide RDs of +0.575 (n=3974) and +0.617 (n=12201). All other leaves have RDs in [-0.07, +0.05]. The tree's structure independently confirms the manually constructed subgroup definition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The presence of any one of mcrpc=1, ar_v7_positive=1, brca2_mutation=1, or msi_high=1 is sufficient to suppress the enzalutamide benefit; the absence of all four is necessary for the benefit.

analyses.code: Stratified TE in 'any of 4 modifiers present' vs 'none present'
analyses.result_summary: Any modifier present (n=34319): enza RD=+0.008 (p=0.06). None present (n=15681): enza RD=+0.626 (p<1e-300). Each individual stratification (BRCA2+ within full cohort, MSI+ within full cohort, etc.) yields RD<0.05. Conclusion: each negative modifier is independently sufficient to abolish the effect; the 4-criterion conjunction is necessary.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL: In male prostate-cancer patients meeting ALL of mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0, treatment_enzalutamide produces a large positive increase in objective_response (risk difference ≈ +0.63, OR ≈ 20 after adjustment); patients failing any one of these four predicates derive essentially no enzalutamide benefit.

FINAL: In this cohort, treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, treatment_olaparib, treatment_lu177_psma, and treatment_pembrolizumab show no main effect on objective_response, no clinically meaningful subgroup with positive effect (including the canonical biomarker matches BRCA2/olaparib, MSI-high/pembrolizumab, PSMA-high/lu177-PSMA), and no benefit when added to enzalutamide.

analyses.code: Final adjusted logistic on the responder subgroup, with all covariates and treatments.
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup n=15681, response rate 42.4%. Enzalutamide on: 79.8% vs off: 17.2%. Crude RD=+0.626 (p<1e-300). Adjusted OR=20.5 (coef=+3.02, p<1e-300) controlling for age, ECOG, PSA, albumin, CRP, weight loss, visceral_mets, PSMA, Gleason, and the other five treatments. Outside the subgroup, RD=+0.008 (p=0.06).

Adjusted ORs (whole cohort): abiraterone 0.97, docetaxel 1.00, olaparib 1.03, lu177-PSMA 1.03, pembrolizumab 1.01 — all p>0.3. Within plausibly responsive subgroups: olaparib in BRCA2+ RD=-0.032 (refutes positive direction), pembrolizumab in MSI-high RD=+0.001, lu177 in PSMA-high RD=-0.001. Within enzalutamide-responder subgroup: all five additional treatments have coefficients with |coef|<0.08 and p>0.10. No alternative treatment-by-subgroup signal survives scrutiny.
